steps.title: Apply the triangle inequality
steps.content: The triangle inequality theorem requires the sum of any two sides to be greater than the third side. With two sides of length 8 and 13, and unknown third side $x$, set up three inequalities: $8+13\gt x$, $8+x\gt 13$, and $13+x\gt 8$.

steps.title: Solve each inequality
steps.content:
From $8+13\gt x$: $x\lt 21$.

From $8+x\gt 13$: $x\gt 5$.

From $13+x\gt 8$: $x\gt -5$, which is always satisfied for positive $x$.

steps.title: Combine constraints
steps.content: Combining the binding constraints gives $5\lt x\lt 21$.

steps.title: Evaluate the choices
steps.content:
• A ($x\lt 5$): gives only values too small - eliminated.
  • B ($x\gt 21$): gives only values too large - eliminated.
  • C ($5\lt x\lt 21$): matches both constraints exactly. ✓
  • D ($x\lt 5$ or $x\gt 21$): these are the excluded regions, not the valid range - eliminated.
C is correct.
